content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 31, 1991

John R. Valencia
Wilke, Fleury, Hoffelt,
Gould & Birney
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 1300
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-332

Dear Mr. Valencia:

You have requested advice under the lobbying disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act 1/ on behalf of your client,
Ms. Patricia Trifunov, a registered lobbyist who is also the
Regional Government Affairs Manager for Glaxo Inc.

QUESTION

Did Ms. Trifunov provide or arrange for a gift to a
legislative official?

CONCLUSION

Ms. Trifunov did not make or arrange for a gift to Ms.
Mitchell and has no obligation to report the food and beverage
consumed by Ms. Mitchell.

FACTS

Patricia Trifunov is a registered in-house lobbyist for Glaxo
Inc. She has organized a program called "Eating for Etiquette" in
which restaurants provide, at no cost, a formal multi-course meal
for minority youth and adult mentors. The mentors discuss
behavior, etiquette and their careers with the youth.

Recently, Ms. Trifunov arranged for an "Eating for Etiquette"
meal at the Firehouse Restaurant. One of the mentors at this
session was Holly Mitchell, a consultant with the Senate Committee

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Valencia, John
Page 2


on Health and Human Services. Ms. Mitchell assisted in
supervising the youth and provided instruction to them in the area
of restaurant behavior and etiquette. She also discussed her own
career and provided career guidance to the youth. The Firehouse
Restaurant provided dinners worth $19.95 to each diner. Ms.
Trifunov provided restaurant service personnel with a gratuity of
$20.00, which added $2.50 to the value of each meal and brought
the total value of the meal to $22.45.

ANALYSIS

Regulation 18728(a) states:

....free admission, food, beverages and similar
nominal benefits provided to a filer at an event at
which he or she speaks, participates in a panel or
seminar or performs a similar service and
reimbursement or advance for actual intrastate
travel and for necessary accommodations provided
directly in connection with the event are not
payments and need not be reported by the filer.

Emphasis added.

It appears that the service performed by Ms. Mitchell at the
"Eating for Etiquette" program would fall under the category of
"performs a similar service." Therefore, the food and beverage
she was provided would not be reportable as either a gift or
honorarium.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

sections.analysis:
Regulation 18728(a) states:

...free admission, food, beverages and similar nominal benefits provided to a filer at an event at which he or she speaks, participates in a panel or seminar or performs a similar service and reimbursement or advance for actual intrastate travel and for necessary accommodations provided directly in connection with the event are not payments and need not be reported by the filer.

Emphasis added.

It appears that the service performed by Ms. Mitchell at the "Eating for Etiquette" program would fall under the category of "performs a similar service." Therefore, the food and beverage she was provided would not be reportable as either a gift or honorarium.
